visual_enhancers.surface_strategy: Clay/Tactile UI - Elements should feel like physical, chunky, pushable wooden toys or plastic blocks.

button_styles.base: relative rounded-[2rem] font-black transition-all active:translate-y-2 select-none flex items-center justify-center gap-3 border-4 border-white/20

visual_enhancers.card_styles: bg-white rounded-[2.5rem] p-8 md:p-12 shadow-[0_16px_40px_rgba(7,59,76,0.06)] border-4 border-white overflow-hidden relative
visual_enhancers.borders: Avoid thin 1px sharp borders. Use thick 4px-8px strokes (white or tinted) or rely entirely on colored clay shadows for structure.
visual_enhancers.decorations: Backgrounds should feature subtle, oversized SVG geometric shapes (circles, wavy blobs, stars) with 5-10% opacity floating around to break up the flat cream background.

layouts.container: max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 py-8 md:px-12 md:py-16
layouts.navigation: Bottom-heavy navigation for tiny hands. Top bar should only contain a massive 'Back' arrow and current Star/Badge count. No complex hamburger menus.

motion.entrance: Staggered spring entrances using framer-motion: initial={{ scale: 0.5, opacity: 0, rotate: -5 }} animate={{ scale: 1, opacity: 1, rotate: 0 }} transition={{ type: 'spring', bounce: 0.6, duration: 0.8 }}
motion.hover: Playful scale up (scale: 1.05) and slight rotation (rotate: 2deg).
motion.tap: Scale down (0.95) and translate-y to simulate pushing a physical block.
motion.feedback_success: Massive bounce + glow. Use react-confetti for module completion.
motion.feedback_error: Gentle horizontal wiggle (x: [-10, 10, -10, 10, 0]) to indicate 'try again' rather than a harsh error.

accessibility.touch_targets: Massive. Minimum 64x64 pixels. The entire card should be clickable, not just the button inside it.
accessibility.contrast: Deep indigo text (#073B4C) on yellow or white backgrounds ensures high APCA readability for developing eyes.
accessibility.feedback: Visual feedback is non-negotiable. Every tap MUST have a visual reaction.
accessibility.data_testid: Required on all interactive elements. Example: data-testid='module-counting-button'

media.icons: Use lucide-react. Standardize at size 48 to 80, with strokeWidth 3 or 4. Often nest them inside a circular contrasting background to make them 'pop'.
media.images: Prioritize high-quality 3D renders or vector graphics. UI must not rely on reading.

3d_libraries.recommendation: Use framer-motion for all UI interactivity. Use react-confetti for rewards. Complex WebGL/Three.js is overkill and distracts from the core learning loops; stick to 2.5D tactile clay shadows.

image_urls.description: Green toy dinosaur - Use as the app's friendly mascot on the home page and as a visual guide or cheerleader inside the learning modules.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. Setup Tailwind with the custom colors: primary, secondary, accent_pink, accent_green, background, and text.
2. Add Google Fonts 'Fredoka' and 'Nunito' to the global CSS and configure them in Tailwind config (sans).

3. Implement the 'Clay UI' button style. Use raw HTML/Tailwind for game modules. Avoid standard Shadcn Cards or Buttons, as they are too corporate/sterile for a 5-year-old. Create custom tactile buttons with thick colored bottom shadows (e.g. shadow-[0_8px_0_#E5B64E]) that reduce to 0px on active/tap state to simulate a real button press.
4. Text MUST be large, minimal, and friendly. Avoid standard paragraphs. Use icons + short labels (e.g., 'Play!', 'Numbers').
5. For the games (counting, shapes): Create large draggable or clickable zones. Use framer-motion for success/failure feedback (wiggle on wrong, bounce + glow on correct).
6. Build a Progress/Rewards page utilizing SVGs (Lucide 'Star' or 'Award') for stars/badges. When a user earns a star, trigger `react-confetti`.
7. Use lucide-react for icons, but make them massive (size 48-64) with strokeWidth 3 or 4.
8. The app background should be the soft cream (#FCF9F2). Optionally, add scattered faint SVG shapes (circles, triangles) with low opacity to make it feel like a colorful playroom.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and clay-like shadows.
Do not center align the App container globally (.App { text-align: center; } is FORBIDDEN).
All interactive elements must have micro-animations (hover/active states).
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Never use generic AI assistant emoji characters for icons. Use lucide-react.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
